paper_title: The Clarkia Age Model and Implications

methods.description: This method involves constructing an age model for the Clarkia deposit by analyzing sedimentation rates, varve structures, and U-Pb zircon ages. The age model helps in understanding the depositional environment changes and the evolutionary history of the Clarkia paleolake.

steps.description: Evidence of varved structures found throughout the site P-33 section and their sedimentation rate variation is crucial to reconstruct an age model associated with the change of depositional environments for the Clarkia deposit.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze varve structures and sedimentation rates to reconstruct age model

steps.description: Assuming similar sedimentation rates for the transitional Unit 3 and instantaneous deposition of autochthonous ash layers, the ∼7.5 m sedimentary sequence at site P-33 corresponds to ∼840 varve years.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate varve years from sedimentary sequence and ash layers

steps.description: The U-Pb ages from the ash layers, although not precise enough to calibrate the age model, do help to place the Clarkia deposits into the overall temporal framework of Columbia River Basalt Group eruption.
steps.searchable_summary: use U-Pb ages to place deposits in temporal framework

methods.description: This method involves analyzing shifts in sedimentation rates at site P-33 using spectral analysis of μ-XRF data to understand changes in the drainage system and lake stratification.

steps.description: Shifts in sedimentation rates at site P-33 detected via spectral analysis of μ-XRF data are consistent with the proposed sudden changes in the drainage system.
steps.searchable_summary: perform spectral analysis of μ-XRF data to detect sedimentation rate shifts
